product_description: Coin Identifier is a mobile app that helps users identify coins by scanning them with a phone camera. The app allows users to search for coins, scan or identify a coin, view coin details, see overview information, check a reference price range, and save identified coins to a personal collection called My Coins. The app is designed for casual users, beginners, and coin collectors who want a quick and simple way to learn more about old or unfamiliar coins. The app should be presented as a helpful coin identification and reference tool. Use safe wording such as reference price range, estimated value, coin details, and coin information. Do not claim that the app can identify every coin, provide an exact price, guarantee authenticity, detect counterfeits, guarantee resale value, or replace professional grading.
brief: Brief: Create a vertical short direct-response ad for a coin scanning app. The ad must follow this story closely: there is one old coin on a table; a foreign/Western-looking adult male actor, not Vietnamese, picks it up with curious confidence and wonders what coin it is; he opens the app, scans both sides of the coin, then shows the result screen with a high reference range. He calls loudly toward someone off-frame: ¡Mira esto! He smiles with a playful victorious expression, turns the phone toward the camera to show the result clearly, then points downward as a download CTA. Use one primary male actor only. Do not show the off-frame person. Make the actor look like a believable foreign app-commercial actor, not Vietnamese. Use English spoken dialogue, except the exact Spanish phrase ¡Mira esto! where requested. - Optional product context: The product is a mobile coin identification and collection app. Uploaded product reference 1 is the result/detail screen: it shows a 1909-S VDB Lincoln Cent detail page with coin photos, title, categories, tabs, Overview fields, a Reference Price section showing $100,000 - $500,000, a disclaimer that value may vary, and a yellow Save to My Coins button. Uploaded product reference 2 is the scan/camera screen: it shows Step 1, instruction to arrange the obverse side centrally, a circular coin guide, crosshair, OBVERSE and REVERSE controls, a large yellow capture button, Photos and Snap Tips controls, close and flash icons. The ad should preserve the yellow brand UI, readable app screens, scan flow, and result/detail screen. Do not claim guaranteed value, appraisal, payout, auction, sale, or authentication. Treat the displayed range as a reference estimate only.

vision_analysis.detected_visual_style: Clean, functional, informative, user-friendly, digital interface
notes: Analysis based solely on textual descriptions provided in the project brief and product details, as no image files were uploaded.

productAnalysis.coreBenefit: Identify unknown coins quickly and get an estimated reference price range.
doNotAssume: Any specific value beyond the provided reference range
productAnalysis.productLockPrompt: Preserve the app's yellow UI, readable scan screen with circular guide and yellow capture button, and readable result screen showing 'Reference Price' with a range like '$100,000 - $500,000' and a 'Save to My Coins' button. Maintain the specified scan flow.

productReferences.name: Coin Identifier App Screens
productReferences.visualDescription: This reference describes two key app screens: a scan/camera screen (Step 1, instructions, circular coin guide, crosshair, OBVERSE/REVERSE controls, large yellow capture button) and a result/detail screen (1909-S VDB Lincoln Cent detail page, coin photos, title, categories, tabs, Overview fields, a Reference Price section showing '$100,000 - $500,000', a disclaimer 'value may vary', and a yellow 'Save to My Coins' button). Both screens feature yellow brand UI elements.
productReferences.lockPrompt: Ensure the app's yellow UI, the circular coin guide, crosshair, OBVERSE/REVERSE controls, yellow capture button on the scan screen, and the readable result screen with 'Reference Price' showing '$100,000 - $500,000' and 'Save to My Coins' button are accurately depicted. Maintain the disclaimer 'value may vary'.
productReferences.useWhen: When showing the app's scanning process or displaying identified coin details and reference price.

scenes.title: Curious Discovery & Scan
scenes.sceneGoal: Show the actor finding an old coin, expressing curiosity, and then using the app to scan it, highlighting the ease of use.
scenes.visualAction: A weathered old coin sits on a simple table. A foreign/Western-looking adult male hand enters the frame, picks up the coin, turning it over to examine it. The actor's face shows curious confidence as he wonders about the coin. He pulls out his smartphone, opens the Coin Identifier app, places the coin on the table, and aligns the obverse side within the circular guide on the app's scan screen. He taps the yellow capture button. He then flips the coin, aligns the reverse side, and taps the capture button again.
scenes.productMoment: The physical old coin is shown, then the app's scan screen (obverse side, then reverse side) is clearly visible on the phone, preserving the yellow UI, circular guide, and yellow capture button.
scenes.characterAction: Actor picks up coin with curious confidence, examines it, opens the app, carefully positions the coin for scanning, and interacts with the app's capture buttons.
scenes.locationUse: A simple, clean tabletop setting, possibly a home or study, with good lighting to highlight the coin and phone.

camera.selected: Stable eye-level medium shot transitioning to a phone close-up
camera.shot: Medium shot of actor and coin, then close-up on phone screen
camera.movement: Starts with a slight push-in on the coin, then a smooth pan/tilt to follow the actor's hand to the phone, ending with a close-up on the phone screen as he scans.
camera.composition: Coin and actor's expression are clear initially, then the app's scan UI is fully readable and central during the scanning action.
alternatives: Over-the-shoulder shot of actor using phone
Direct overhead shot of coin being scanned

voiceLines.speaker: Primary actor
voiceLines.actionState: Examining the coin with a thoughtful expression.
voiceLines.line: Hmm, this looks really old. I wonder what it is?

voiceLines.speaker: Primary actor
voiceLines.actionState: Opening the app and starting the scan.
voiceLines.line: Let's see what the Coin Identifier says.

scenes.ambientAudio: Subtle room tone, sound of coin being picked up, gentle app opening sound, distinct camera shutter sound for each scan.
timingBeats: 0-2s: Old coin on table, actor's hand enters and picks it up.
5-6s: Actor pulls out phone, opens app.
6-9s: Actor places coin, scans obverse and reverse, speaks line 2.
9-10s: Final scan completes, app transitions to processing (briefly).

keyframePrompts.label: Actor discovering the coin
keyframePrompts.purpose: Establish the initial curiosity and the physical coin.
keyframePrompts.prompt: Vertical video, eye-level medium shot. A foreign/Western-looking adult male actor, not Vietnamese, with curious confidence, picks up an old, weathered coin from a simple wooden table. His face shows intrigue. The coin is clearly visible in his hand. Natural lighting.

keyframePrompts.label: Scanning the obverse side
keyframePrompts.purpose: Show the app's scan screen and the first step of identification.
keyframePrompts.prompt: Vertical video, close-up shot on a smartphone screen. The foreign/Western-looking male actor's hands hold the phone, displaying the Coin Identifier app's scan screen. The screen clearly shows 'Step 1', instructions to arrange the obverse side centrally, a circular coin guide, crosshair, 'OBVERSE' control, and a large yellow capture button. An old coin is perfectly aligned within the guide. The app's yellow UI is prominent and readable.

scenes.finalVideoPrompt: Create a 10-second vertical video. Scene starts with an eye-level medium shot of a foreign/Western-looking adult male actor, not Vietnamese, picking up an old coin from a table, expressing curious confidence. He says, <Hmm, this looks really old. I wonder what it is?>. Camera smoothly transitions to a close-up on his smartphone as he opens the Coin Identifier app. He places the coin on the table and scans its obverse side, then flips it and scans the reverse side, interacting with the app's yellow capture button. He says, <Let's see what the Coin Identifier says.>. Preserve the app's yellow UI, readable scan screen with circular guide, crosshair, OBVERSE/REVERSE controls, and yellow capture button, as shown in the visual ingredients mapped by keyframe prompts. Use natural lighting. Overlay text: 'Identify any coin!'. Visual ingredients: kf_coin_discovery (0-3s), kf_scan_obverse (6-8s). Negative rules: do not redesign the product/app, no unreadable UI text, no off-frame person visible, actor must not be Vietnamese.

scenes.title: Shocking Result & CTA
scenes.sceneGoal: Showcase the app's ability to provide significant information (high reference range) and prompt the user to download.
scenes.visualAction: The actor's phone screen displays the Coin Identifier app's result screen, showing a high reference price range (e.g., $100,000 - $500,000). The actor's eyes widen slightly in surprise, then a playful victorious smile spreads across his face. He looks off-frame, calls loudly: <¡Mira esto!>. He then turns the phone directly towards the camera, ensuring the result screen is clearly visible and readable. Finally, he points downward, indicating a download CTA.
scenes.productMoment: The app's result/detail screen is prominently displayed, with the 'Reference Price' section and the high range clearly readable. The yellow 'Save to My Coins' button is also visible.
scenes.characterAction: Actor reacts with surprise and joy, calls out, proudly displays the phone, and performs a clear download gesture.
scenes.locationUse: Same simple, clean tabletop setting, maintaining continuity.

camera.selected: Close-up on phone screen transitioning to a medium shot of actor
camera.shot: Close-up on phone, then medium shot of actor
camera.movement: Starts with a static close-up on the phone screen, then a quick pull-back to a medium shot of the actor as he reacts and speaks, ending with a slight push-in on the phone as he turns it to the camera.
camera.composition: App screen is fully readable, then actor's expression and gesture are clear, then app screen is readable again for CTA.
alternatives: Over-the-shoulder shot of actor reacting to phone

voiceLines.speaker: Primary actor
voiceLines.actionState: Looking off-frame with a playful victorious smile.

voiceLines.speaker: Primary actor
voiceLines.actionState: Turning phone to camera and pointing downward.
voiceLines.line: You have to try this app!

scenes.ambientAudio: Room tone, actor's excited exclamation, subtle triumphant music cue, gentle whoosh sound for CTA pointer.
timingBeats: 0-3s: Phone screen shows result, actor reacts with surprise and smile.
4-6s: Actor turns phone to camera, showing result clearly.
6-8s: Actor points downward, speaks CTA line.

keyframePrompts.label: App result screen with high value
keyframePrompts.purpose: Showcase the app's ability to provide valuable information clearly.
keyframePrompts.prompt: Vertical video, close-up shot on a smartphone screen. The Coin Identifier app's result/detail screen is clearly visible, showing '1909-S VDB Lincoln Cent', coin photos, and a prominent 'Reference Price' section displaying '$100,000 - $500,000'. The disclaimer 'value may vary' and the yellow 'Save to My Coins' button are also readable. The app's yellow UI is preserved.

keyframePrompts.label: Actor showing phone and CTA
keyframePrompts.purpose: Conclude with a clear display of the app's value and a direct call to action.
keyframePrompts.prompt: Vertical video, medium shot. The foreign/Western-looking adult male actor, not Vietnamese, with a playful victorious expression, holds his smartphone with the Coin Identifier app's result screen clearly facing the camera. The screen shows the high reference price range. The actor points downward with his other hand, indicating a download. The app's yellow UI is visible and readable.

scenes.finalVideoPrompt: Create an 8-second vertical video. Scene starts with a close-up on the Coin Identifier app's result screen on a smartphone, clearly showing the 'Reference Price' of '$100,000 - $500,000' and 'value may vary' disclaimer. Camera pulls back to a medium shot of the foreign/Western-looking adult male actor, not Vietnamese, reacting with a playful victorious smile. He calls out loudly, <¡Mira esto!>. He then turns the phone directly to the camera, ensuring the result screen is readable, and points downward, saying, <You have to try this app!>. Preserve the app's yellow UI, readable result screen, and the 'Save to My Coins' button, as shown in the visual ingredients mapped by keyframe prompts. Use natural lighting. Overlay text: 'Download now!'. Visual ingredients: kf_result_screen (0-2s), kf_actor_cta (6-8s). Negative rules: do not redesign the product/app, no unreadable UI text, no off-frame person visible, actor must not be Vietnamese.
